body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: ready

Route: record. This OVERTURNS the prior gap:slash-command verdict, which was written before PR #495 (2026-05-26) added a live-TUI driver path to drive-opencode-interactive.sh.

## Scenario

synchronous-slash-command: a ready session invokes a slash command that responds LOCALLY without calling the model; the session stays ready throughout the slash window with NO -> working transition recorded for the slash command itself (scenario-meanings User-observable signal). The whole observable is the ABSENCE of a second working transition. Primitive exercised: slash_commands. Distinct from context-compaction (/compact DOES call the model) and session-reset (/new ends the session).

## Agent supports it (agent_supports: yes)

opencode's interactive TUI ships in-process commands that respond without an LLM round-trip. `strings` over the bundled binary (/Users/ingo/.opencode/bin/opencode, v1.14.50) surfaces the command registry: help, compact, details, editor, exit, init, messages, models, new, redo, sessions, share, themes, undo. Confirmed LIVE this sweep: typing `/help` in the TUI pops the command autocomplete (`/help  Help`) — opencode treats it as a recognized in-TUI command, not as prompt text.

## daemon observes it (daemon_capability: full)

opencode is the lone ProcessOwnedStore adapter (core/adapters/inbound/agents/opencode/agent.go); the daemon derives session state from the SQLite store. State is PART-DRIVEN: the watcher's scanParts (core/adapters/inbound/agents/opencode/watcher.go ~L473-533) emits EventActivity only when a NEW `part` row lands; with no new part there is no activity event and the session stays ready.

Proven LIVE this sweep: after a warm-up turn (step-finish=1, 4 parts, 2 messages), delivering `/help` to the live TUI exactly as the driver's `slash` step does (tmux send-keys -l -- "/help" then Enter) wrote NOTHING to the store — parts stayed 4, step-finish stayed 1, messages stayed 2, user-messages stayed 1, and NO new session was minted. The only stored user-message text remained `say hi in one word` (the warm-up); `/help` was never persisted as a prompt. So the daemon sees zero new activity across the slash window and the session stays ready — exactly the read-from-store no-op basic-turn / multi-turn already prove. The daemon is fully capable.

## driver can record it (driver_capability: ready)

The opencode driver gained a LIVE-TUI path in PR #495 (commit 295fbbfe, 2026-05-26 20:09). drive-opencode-interactive.sh now routes any recipe carrying a `reset_session` OR `slash` step to run_live() (lines 352-357), which launches the opencode TUI under tmux, runs warm-up turns via send-keys, and delivers slash commands to the LIVE held-open session (the `slash` case at lines 205-210: `tmux send-keys -l -- "$s"` then Enter). Turn detection polls step-finish counts in the SQLite store; both pre- and post- sessions are exported via the multi-session contract.

This is the SAME primitive the sibling opencode session-reset cell now uses — and session-reset was successfully RECORDED via this path (replaydata/agents/opencode/scenarios/session-reset/{events,transcript}.jsonl + golden; manifest 7/7 phases passed, daemon 0.4.7). A synchronous no-LLM slash (/help) is delivered by the identical step type; this sweep's live probe confirms it produces the no-store-write, stays-ready behavior the scenario asserts. The driver has every step type the recipe needs (send -> warm-up turn; slash -> /help; sleep -> dwell), so driver_capability is ready.

## Why headless is NOT the path (and why the prior verdict is stale)

The prior assessment (and the scenarios.json/coverage notes) said the driver had ONLY send|wait_turn|sleep and that headless `opencode run` stores slash commands as literal user text. The headless finding is still TRUE — confirmed this sweep: `opencode run -- "/help"` stored `/help` as a literal user prompt, ran a full model turn (step-finish=1), and the model replied to it as text. Headless would invert the scenario (ready->working->ready WITH a model call). But this no longer matters: the driver routes slash steps to the LIVE-TUI path, never headless `run`. The `slash` step + run_live() did not exist when the prior verdict was written; the gap:slash-command label is now stale.

## What recording would look like

A recipe with: send (warm-up one-word turn) -> wait_turn -> slash (/help) -> sleep (dwell). The driver's slash router fires run_live; expected.jsonl asserts the warm-up working->ready arc, then the session stays ready across the /help window with NO further state_transition to working/waiting and no second process turn — the committed expected.jsonl already documents this arc.

## Matrix drift

The coverage matrix records opencode/synchronous-slash-command as driver_capability:gap:slash-command (notes cite #492 'needs a live-REPL opencode driver'). PR #495 delivered exactly that live-REPL driver. The cell should be updated to driver_capability:ready (agent_supports:yes, daemon_capability:full unchanged). The whole opencode gap:slash-command cluster (session-reset already flipped to ready; checkpoint-rewind, context-compaction) should be re-examined against the new live path.

## Confidence

0.9: agent_supports:yes is the binary command registry + a live command-popup observation; daemon_capability:full is grounded in the part-driven scanParts read PLUS a live store probe showing /help writes zero parts; driver_capability:ready is grounded in the committed live-TUI `slash` step, the SUCCESSFULLY RECORDED sibling session-reset via the same path, and this sweep's live /help probe. Not 0.95 only because this exact cell has not yet been end-to-end recorded+validated (that is the implement stage).
caveats: Headless `opencode run` is NOT the recording path for this cell and must not be: `opencode run -- "/help"` stores `/help` as a literal user prompt and runs a model turn (confirmed this sweep), which would invert the scenario. The driver correctly routes any `slash` step to the live-TUI tmux path (run_live), so a recipe MUST use a `slash` step, never a `send` step, to deliver the slash command.

sources.note: run_live() live-TUI path (lines 99-294) with a `slash` step (lines 205-210: tmux send-keys -l -- "$s" then Enter against the live held-open opencode TUI); the dispatch at lines 352-357 routes any reset_session/slash step to run_live. Added in PR #495 (commit 295fbbfe, 2026-05-26) — the driver now HAS the slash primitive the prior verdict said was missing.

scanParts (~L473-533) is part-driven: it emits EventActivity only when a NEW part row lands. A no-LLM /help writes no new part, so no activity event fires and the session stays ready — daemon_capability: full.

opencode is the lone ProcessOwnedStore adapter; the daemon reads session state from SQLite via ComputeMetrics/the watcher. A local slash with no new assistant turn leaves the store unchanged, so the session stays ready (read-from-store no-op).

sibling opencode slash-driven cell SUCCESSFULLY RECORDED via the same live-TUI path (PR #495); manifest reports 7/7 phases passed. Proves the live-TUI slash primitive works end-to-end — synchronous-slash-command uses the identical `slash` step type.

v1.14.50; `strings` surfaces the in-TUI command registry (help, compact, models, new, undo, redo, sessions, share, themes, ...). Live probe: typing /help pops the `/help  Help` command autocomplete — opencode treats it as a recognized no-LLM in-TUI command, agent_supports: yes.
committed target arc — a warm-up turn, then the session stays ready across the slash window with NO state_transition to working/waiting for the local command.
coverage cell currently driver_capability:gap:slash-command (#492 'needs a live-REPL opencode driver') — STALE; PR #495 delivered that driver. Should update to driver_capability:ready.
